program_en: Articulated Bachelor of Science – Ph.D. Programme in Mathematical Sciences
program_chi: 數學科學理學士哲學博士銜接課程
learning_outcomes:
1.  |  Undergraduate Study
Upon completion of this
Programme, students will have acquired:
Knowledge outcomes
(1)  |  Linear Algebra and Abstract
Algebra;
(2)  |  Analysis which includes
Calculus, Real Analysis, Complex Analysis, Fourier Analysis and Functional
Analysis;
(3)  |  Differential Geometry;
(4)  |  Computer programming for
Mathematics and Numerical Analysis; and
(5)  |  Physics which includes classic
mechanics, electromagnetism, fluids, waves, optics, and quantum mechanics
Skills outcomes
Generic Competencies
(1)  |  An ability to understand broad
mathematical literature independently;
(2)  |  An awareness of mathematical
problems arising in generic situations;
(3)  |  An ability to communicate
mathematical problems effectively;
(4)  |  An ability to formulate
mathematical problems and know how to find ways to solve them; and
(5)  |  An ability to solve real-life
problems with mathematical software.
Sectors of Professional Skills
In addition, students will
have possessed professional skills in at least one sector:
(1)  |  Postgraduate research
(2)  |  Teaching
(3)  |  Interdisciplinary research
between mathematics and other scientific disciplines
Value and attitudes
outcomes
(1)  |  An ability to apply their
mathematical knowledge generically in their future profession in a way that
is unique for mathematically trained experts; and
(2)  |  An ability to make important
original contributions in mathematical sciences and lead breakthrough of
interdisciplinary mathematical-science applications.
Advanced Learning Opportunity
(1)  |  An ability to take the
Candidacy Examination for the Ph.D. programme of the Articulated Bachelor
of Science – Ph.D. Programme in Mathematical Sciences (ABP).
(2)  |  An ability to transit into the
Ph.D. programme of ABP Programme.
(3)  |  An ability to prepare research
proposal and decide the research topics in the Ph.D. programme of ABP
Programme.
2.  |  Research Postgraduate Study
(1)  |  Our research programme aims to
nurture talented and committed students who have the capability and
aspiration to embark on careers that would allow them to become world leaders
in mathematical sciences to contribute to the development of Hong Kong, the
Country, and the international community.
(2)  |  The University expects
doctoral degree graduates of research programmes to have acquired in-depth knowledge in a number of major areas of an
academic discipline while maintaining a broad understanding of other related
fields. Doctoral degree graduates should have accumulated enough educational
experience and background learning to be capable of performing independent
research to advance scholarship, with global standards. In particular,
doctoral graduates should have the ability to identify research trends and
opportunities, venture into new research areas when appropriate, define
long-term research objectives, formulate original research problems, and
originate and develop solution methodologies. Doctoral graduates should be
capable of producing research output at a level that can either lead to
publications in high-ranking scholastic venues, or to novel applications in
relevant industrial, commercial, or other public sectors, or to other forms
of useful knowledge transfer to society. They should have gained proficiency
in techniques of knowledge dissemination through presentation and writing and
some teaching experiences through student tutoring.
(3)  |  For graduates of research
programmes at doctoral level, communication and language skills at a level
appropriate to university graduates are expected already at the time of
admission. In particular, fluent communication skills are expected in the
language(s) essential to their research areas. In general, a high level of
proficiency in English is expected as it is commonly regarded as the default
international research language. Ability in a second language is encouraged.
(4)  |  Graduates of the ABP Programme
will build collaborations between physics, computer science and engineering
through the application of advanced mathematical techniques. They will bridge
the gap between mathematics and innovative applications. They will drive
breakthroughs at the interface between theory and practice by combining deep
mathematical rigor with the communicative agility to lead interdisciplinary
teams. The ABP Programme’s accelerated course of study ensures that graduates
excel in both specialized research and interdisciplinary problem-solving,
preparing them to tackle complex challenges in academia and industry.
study_scheme:
Artificial Intelligence: Systems and Technologies Articulated
Bachelor of Science – Ph.D. Programme in Mathematical Sciences Applicable to
students admitted in 2025-26
Bachelor of Science in
Mathematical Sciences
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 71 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
IMSC1038,
1113, 1122
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  41
(a)  |  IMSC2018, 2028, 2048, 2058, 2068, MATH2070/2078,
2221, 2230, PHYS2041, 3011, 3021, 4011, 4021
(b)  |  Capstone course: MATH4400 or 4900[a]
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  21
(a) (b) (c) (d) (e) (f) (g)  |  3 units from MATH3030, 5051 3 units from MATH3040, 4080 3 units from MATH3093, 4060 3 units from MATH3230, 5211 3 units from MATH4030, 5061 3 units from MATH4050, 5011 3 units from MATH4220, 5021
Total:  |  71
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  IMSC1038, 1113, 1122, IMSC, MATH and PHYS courses at
2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for
honours classification.
2.  |  Courses at 5000 and 6000
level completed in undergraduate study will be automatically recognised
to fulfill the Coursework Requirement for the Ph.D. Programme. No
application for course and unit exemptions is required.
3.  |  Students will be conferred the Bachelor’s degree,
subject to the fulfillment of the undergraduate graduation requirements.
4.  |  To be eligible to proceed with the postgraduate
studies, students are required to satisfy the minimum admission
requirements prescribed by the Graduate School and Zhizhen School after
completion of the fourth year.
5.  |  Students are required to sit for the
Candidacy Examination in their fourth year of attendance, even if
they have not yet completed their undergraduate studies. They have to
pass the Candidacy Examination before the end of the first year of
attendance of postgraduate study to satisfy the Candidacy Examination
requirement for the Ph.D. Programme. Students may attempt to take the
Candidacy Examination in their third year of attendance subject to
approval of the School Director. Students who win individual gold or
silver awards in the Yau College Student Mathematics Competition or
equivalent may apply for exemption from the Candidacy Examination in the
corresponding direction.
6.  |  In order to fulfill the graduation requirements for
postgraduate study, students must complete a minimum of 9 months of
non-local learning/ research experience, with at least 3 consecutive
months of such experience. A formal term-time exchange programme during
undergraduate study, or non-consecutive and substantial non-local
learning/ research experiences of 4 months or more offered by the
University, Colleges, the Faculty of Engineering or Science, Zhizhen
School, the Mathematics Department and other relevant programmes may also
be considered, subject to the approval of the Division Head.
[a]  |  Upon approval of the School Director, students may
use MATH4900 to fulfill the Capstone Requirement.
Doctor of Philosophy in Mathematical Sciences
Pre-candidacy
The “candidacy requirement” composes of four major
parts, namely coursework requirement, candidacy examination, thesis
proposal (and oral defence) and stipulated Improving Postgraduate
Learning (IPL) module requirements. Students must complete and fulfill
the four parts within the maximum period stipulated by
the Programme. Details of the requirement are listed below:
Units
1.  |  Coursework Requirement
(a)  |  Lecture courses:  |  15
15 units of MATH courses at 5000 and 6000 level, with
at least 9 units of these courses covering at least three of the
following directions:
(i)  |  Algebra and Number Theory
MATH5051, 5052, 6031, 6032, 6061, 6062
(ii)  |  Analysis and Partial Differential Equations
MATH5011, 5012, 5021, 5022, 6041, 6042, 6081, 6082
(iii)  |  Computational and Applied Mathematics
MATH5211, 5212, 6211, 6212, 6221, 6222, 6231, 6232,
6241, 6242, 6251, 6261
(iv)  |  Geometry and Topology
MATH5070, 5031, 5032, 5061, 5062, 6021, 6022, 6051,
6052, 6071, 6072
(b)  |  Thesis research/ monitoring courses:  |  12
Students must register for a Thesis Research course
every term: MATH8006
2.  |  Candidacy Examination
Students need to pass the Candidacy Examination
(written and oral examinations) in 3 directions out of 4. The Candidacy
Examination directions consist of: (a) Algebra and Number Theory, (b) Analysis
and Partial Differential Equations, (c) Computational and Applied
Mathematics, and (d) Geometry and Topology. Those who win individual gold or silver awards in the
Yau College Student Mathematics Competition or equivalent are allowed to
apply for exemption from the Candidacy Examination in the corresponding
direction.
3.  |  Thesis Proposal and Oral Defence
Students must submit a research proposal endorsed by
the supervisor, and pass an oral defence.
4.  |  Improving Postgraduate Learning (IPL) modules:
Students are required to complete and pass the following
Improving Postgraduate Learning (IPL) modules in their first year of
attendance for partial fulfilment of candidacy requirements:
(a)  |  an IPL module on “Basics of Research Data Management” This is an online module and relevant information can
be accessed from the website: https://www.cuhk.edu.hk/clear/download/IPL-Researchskills.pdf.
(b)  |  Students are required to submit a Data Management
Plan (DMP), with effect from the 2025-26 intake.
5.  |  Other Requirements
(a)  |  Students must fulfill the Term Assessment Requirement
of the Graduate School. For details, please refer to Clause 13.0
“Unsatisfactory Performance and Discontinuation of Studies” of the
General Regulations Governing Postgraduate Studies which can be accessed
from the Graduate School Homepage: https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk.
Total:  |  27
Remarks:
1.  |  Ph.D. students are
required to fulfill all of the above candidacy requirements [1 to 5] by
the end of the first year of attendance of postgraduate study before
advancement to Ph.D. post-candidacy.
Post-candidacy
Units
1.  |  Coursework Requirement
(a)  |  Thesis research/ monitoring courses:  |  72
Students must register for a Thesis Research course
every term: MATH8012 (or MATH8006 for continuing students).
(b)  |  Other course(s):  |  6
Students must take Graduate Seminars course twice:
MATH7400.
2.  |  Non-local learning/ research experience
Students must complete a minimum of 9 months of
non-local learning/ research experience, with at least 3 consecutive
months of such experience. A formal term-time exchange programme during
undergraduate study, or non-consecutive and substantial non-local
learning/ research experiences of 4 months or more offered by the
University, Colleges, the Faculty of Engineering or Science, the
Mathematics and other relevant programmes may also be considered, subject
to the approval of the Division Head.
3.  |  Improving Postgraduate Learning (IPL) modules:
Students are required to complete the following IPL
modules for graduation:
(a)  |  An IPL module on “Observing Intellectual Property and
Copyright Law during Research”. This is an online module and relevant
information can be accessed from the website: https://www.cuhk.edu.hk/clear/prodev/ipl.html.
(b)  |  An IPL module on “Becoming an Excellent TA: Professional
Development Course for Teaching Assistants”.
(c)  |  Students must complete an online Research Ethics
Training (RET) module on “Publication Ethics” offered by the Office of
Research and Knowledge Transfer Services (ORKTS) and obtain a valid
Publication Ethics Certificate for graduation. Relevant information can
be accessed from the RET website at https://www.research-ethics.cuhk.edu.hk/web/.
(d)  |  The final DMP should have been shared to the CUHK
community on DMP Tool or the relevant platform that CUHK provides
when submitting the final version of the thesis/portfolio to the Graduate
School. Students are required to have published the data on the CUHK
Research Data Repository or an open data repository with a valid Digital
Object Identifier (DOI) or other forms of permanent identifier when
submitting the final version of the thesis/portfolio. Upon thesis
supervisor’s endorsement, students can decide on how much data from the
research project is to be published.
4.  |  Other Requirements
(a)  |  Students must fulfill the Term Assessment Requirement
of the Graduate School. For details, please refer to Clause 13.0 “Unsatisfactory
Performance and Discontinuation of Studies” of the General Regulations
Governing Postgraduate Studies which can be accessed from the Graduate
School Homepage: https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk.
(b)  |  Students are required to submit a research thesis and
pass an oral defence for graduation.
Total:  |  78
